mitigations.label: string-label-only
mitigations.description: Sets a human-readable market identifier string returned by getMarketId(). Not read by any on-chain fund-moving logic; no impact on existing deposits, borrows, or collateral.

mitigations.label: view-only-no-fund-path
mitigations.description: Sets the data provider address used for off-chain queries (getReserveData, getUserAccountData). Not consumed by any on-chain pool logic for liquidations, borrows, or withdrawals; no impact on existing positions.

mitigations.label: admin-lockout
mitigations.description: renounceOwnership permanently removes the admin/owner, locking all owner-only functions forever. This is an irreversible action that would render governance upgrades and parameter changes impossible. Most Aave contracts accept this through full governance vote + 86400s PayloadsController delay, giving the community time to detect and react.

mitigations.label: admin-transfer
mitigations.description: transferOwnership replaces the current admin/owner with an arbitrary address. If set to a malicious or uncontrolled address, all owner-gated functions are immediately accessible to that address. On contracts under Aave governance, this change requires the standard proposal vote + 86400s execution delay.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: initReserve adds a single new reserve slot to the pool. No existing reserve or user position is altered.

mitigations.label: zero-liquidity guard
mitigations.description: dropReserve requires the reserve to have zero total supplied balance and zero accrued treasury fees (_checkNoSuppliers). In practice no active Aave market with real TVL can be dropped; the call reverts unless both totalSupplied == 0 and accruedToTreasury == 0, confining impact to emptied markets only.

mitigations.label: state-sync only
mitigations.description: syncIndexesState accrues outstanding interest to the reserve liquidity index (updates normalizedIncome and accruedToTreasury). It applies time-elapsed interest that would be applied anyway on the next user interaction; no net new value is created or destroyed.

mitigations.label: state-sync only
mitigations.description: syncRatesState recomputes the variable borrow and liquidity rates using current utilization. It is equivalent to the rate-update step that runs on every supply/borrow/repay. No funds move and no user balance changes.

Changes route through the Aave Governance V3 pipeline: proposal creation → voting period (~7 days) → queue at PayloadsController (minimum EXECUTION_DELAY = 86400 seconds / 1 day) → Executor call. The effective minimum delay from proposal to execution is ~4-8 days, with any community member able to observe and react.

mitigations.label: future-only

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: configureEModeCategoryCollateralBitmap sets which assets are recognized as collateral within an eMode category. Existing positions are not reclassified immediately; only future eMode collateral decisions are affected.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: configureEModeCategoryBorrowableBitmap sets which assets are borrowable within an eMode category. Existing eMode borrows are unaffected; only future borrow initiations in that category change.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: configureEModeCategoryLtvzeroBitmap marks which assets in an eMode category have LTV = 0. Existing positions use the liquidation threshold for health calculation; only new eMode borrows against those assets are blocked.

mitigations.label: zero-ceiling guard
mitigations.description: resetIsolationModeTotalDebt requires that the asset's debt ceiling is already 0 (isolation mode disabled). It can only clear a stale counter on an asset that has had its isolation mode removed by governance; it cannot bypass an active ceiling.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setLiquidationGracePeriod sets a timestamp until which liquidations are paused for a given asset. It provides a grace window after price shocks to allow borrowers to top up collateral; existing balances and accrued interest are unaffected.

mitigations.label: mistakenly-sent only
mitigations.description: rescueTokens (onlyPoolAdmin) on the Pool contract has no underlying-exclusion check, but in Aave V3 user deposits are custodied by the aToken contracts (Pool.supply transfers underlying directly from user to reserveCache.aTokenAddress, not to the Pool). The Pool contract holds no user funds in steady state, so this function can only sweep tokens accidentally sent to the Pool address.

mitigations.label: umbrella-self-funded
mitigations.description: eliminateReserveDeficit (onlyUmbrella) burns the calling Umbrella contract's own aToken balance to write down the reserve's deficit counter. It does not touch depositor balances or draw from outside the Umbrella's own position. The operation is beneficial to the protocol: it reduces bad-debt exposure.

mitigations.label: zero-suppliers guard
mitigations.description: Deactivating a reserve (setReserveActive(asset, false)) calls _checkNoSuppliers, which reverts unless totalSupplied == 0 and accruedToTreasury == 0. No reserve with any user deposits can be deactivated. Re-activating is future-only and does not affect existing positions.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: updateFlashloanPremium changes the flash loan fee rate. The fee applies only to future flash loan transactions; accrued supply positions and outstanding borrows are unaffected.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: initReserves adds new reserve slots to the pool with their initial configuration. No existing reserve or user position is altered; the call only prepares new lending markets.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setReserveBorrowing enables or disables new borrowing on a reserve. Already-borrowed positions continue to exist and accrue interest normally; no existing borrower is liquidated or harmed. The change only affects future borrow initiations.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setReserveFlashLoaning enables or disables flash loans on a reserve. Flash loan fees and existing supply positions are unaffected. Only future flash loan calls are restricted.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setReserveFactor changes the fraction of interest accrued that goes to the treasury. The new factor applies from the next interest accrual event forward; already-accrued interest in aTokens is unchanged.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setBorrowCap sets the maximum total borrow amount for a reserve. Existing borrows remain valid; only new borrows that would exceed the new cap are blocked.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setSupplyCap sets the maximum total supply for a reserve. Existing deposits are unaffected; only new supplies that would exceed the new cap are blocked.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setDebtCeiling adjusts the isolation mode debt ceiling. Existing isolation-mode positions remain valid; only future isolation-mode borrows that would breach the ceiling are restricted.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setBorrowableInIsolation marks or unmarks an asset as borrowable in isolation mode. Existing isolation positions are unaffected; only future isolation borrowing of that asset changes.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setSiloedBorrowing enables or disables siloed borrowing for an asset. Existing positions continue; only the rule for new borrows changes.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setLiquidationProtocolFee changes the share of liquidation bonus that accrues to the protocol. Future liquidations pay the new fee; outstanding collateral positions and debt are unaffected.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setAssetBorrowableInEMode changes whether an asset can be borrowed within a given eMode category. Existing eMode positions are unaffected; only future eMode borrow initiations are constrained.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setAssetCollateralInEMode changes whether an asset is recognized as eMode collateral. Existing positions keep their collateral status; the rule applies only to new eMode configurations.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setReserveInterestRateData updates the interest rate strategy parameters (slopes, optimal usage ratio) for a reserve. The new curve governs future interest accrual; it does not restate past accruals or alter outstanding principal.

setReservePause flips the `paused` config bit on a single reserve. While paused, all reserve operations (supply, withdraw, borrow, repay, liquidations) are blocked — funds are not extracted but become temporarily inaccessible. The pause itself has no on-chain max duration; it can be reverted by the same role or by the pool admin (Aave Governance V3, with delay). Worst-case operational impact is bounded by the main V3 market's whole-pool TVS (the tightest upper bound we expose; per-reserve TVS would be tighter but is not currently discovered).
mitigations.label: pause-bound
future-only
mitigations.description: disableLiquidationGracePeriod removes a pending grace period, re-enabling liquidations. This is a defensive/correction action; it does not lock or redirect funds, and existing balances are unaffected.

setPoolPause flips the `paused` config bit on every reserve in the pool, freezing all supply, withdraw, borrow, repay, and liquidation operations across the market. No fund extraction or devaluation path — funds remain backed 1:1 by underlying aTokens once unpaused. No on-chain max pause duration; reversible by the same role or by the pool admin (Aave Governance V3, with delay). Worst-case operational impact is bounded by the live main V3 market TVS.
mitigations.label: pause-bound
future-only
mitigations.description: setReserveLtvzero sets the LTV to 0 for an asset, preventing new borrowing against it as collateral. Existing borrows secured by this collateral remain and the liquidation threshold (not LTV) still governs their health factor, so no immediate liquidation is triggered.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setAssetLtvzeroInEMode sets LTV to 0 for an asset within a specific eMode category. Existing eMode positions use the liquidation threshold for health factor, not LTV; only new eMode borrows are prevented.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setReserveFreeze prevents new supplies and borrows but leaves withdrawals and repayments fully functional. Existing depositors can always exit and existing borrowers can repay. No committed funds are locked or redirected.

setReservePause flips the `paused` config bit on a single reserve. While paused, all reserve operations (supply, withdraw, borrow, repay, liquidations) are blocked — funds are not extracted but become temporarily inaccessible. The pause itself has no on-chain max duration; it can be reverted by the same role or by the pool admin (Aave Governance V3, with delay). Worst-case operational impact is bounded by the Lido market's whole-pool TVS (the tightest upper bound we expose; per-reserve TVS would be tighter but is not currently discovered).
mitigations.label: pause-bound
future-only

setPoolPause flips the `paused` config bit on every reserve in the pool, freezing all supply, withdraw, borrow, repay, and liquidation operations across the market. No on-chain max pause duration; reversible by the same role or by the pool admin (Aave Governance V3, with delay). Worst-case operational impact is bounded by the live Lido market TVS.

mitigations.label: reserve-takeover

Bounded by the affected Horizon reserve's TVS; we use the largest current Horizon reserve (aHorRwaRLUSD totalSupply) as a worst-case proxy.

In practice no active Aave market with real TVL can be dropped; the call reverts unless both totalSupplied == 0 and accruedToTreasury == 0, confining impact to emptied markets only.

mitigations.label: timelock-delayed
mitigations.description: updateAToken upgrades the AToken implementation for a reserve. As the PoolConfigurator is the immutable proxy admin for AToken contracts, only the PoolConfigurator can call the proxy's upgradeToAndCall. On the main Ethereum market, PoolConfigurator.updateAToken requires POOL_ADMIN_ROLE (Executor only), enforcing full governance + 86400s delay. On the Horizon RWA market the 4/6 multisig holds POOL_ADMIN_ROLE.

mitigations.label: timelock-delayed
mitigations.description: updateVariableDebtToken upgrades the VariableDebtToken implementation for a reserve. Same path as updateAToken — requires POOL_ADMIN_ROLE on the PoolConfigurator, which on the main Ethereum market is Executor-only (full governance + 86400s delay). On the Horizon RWA market the 4/6 multisig also holds POOL_ADMIN_ROLE.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setReserveBorrowing enables or disables new borrowing on a reserve. Already-borrowed positions continue to exist and accrue interest normally; no existing borrower is liquidated or harmed. The change only affects future borrow initiations.

mitigations.label: governance-delayed
mitigations.description: configureReserveAsCollateral sets LTV, liquidation threshold, and liquidation bonus for a reserve. Lowering the liquidation threshold below positions' current health factors would trigger cascading liquidations for existing borrowers. On the main Ethereum market this requires full governance vote + 86400s PayloadsController delay.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setReserveFlashLoaning enables or disables flash loans on a reserve. Flash loan fees and existing supply positions are unaffected. Only future flash loan calls are restricted.

mitigations.label: zero-suppliers guard
mitigations.description: Deactivating a reserve (setReserveActive(asset, false)) calls _checkNoSuppliers, which reverts unless totalSupplied == 0 and accruedToTreasury == 0. No reserve with any user deposits can be deactivated. Re-activating is future-only and does not affect existing positions.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setBorrowableInIsolation marks or unmarks an asset as borrowable in isolation mode. Existing isolation positions are unaffected; only future isolation borrowing of that asset changes.

mitigations.label: emergency-pause
mitigations.description: setReservePause pauses all operations (supply, borrow, repay, withdraw, liquidation) for a specific reserve. Pausing blocks user withdrawals. On the main Ethereum market, this can be triggered by any POOL_ADMIN or EMERGENCY_ADMIN (Aave Guardian). The Aave Guardian multisig can pause without a governance vote; the Executor (governance) can also pause.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: disableLiquidationGracePeriod removes a pending grace period, re-enabling liquidations. This is a defensive/correction action; it does not lock or redirect funds, and existing balances are unaffected.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setReserveFactor changes the fraction of interest accrued that goes to the treasury. The new factor applies from the next interest accrual event forward; already-accrued interest in aTokens is unchanged.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setDebtCeiling adjusts the isolation mode debt ceiling. Existing isolation-mode positions remain valid; only future isolation-mode borrows that would breach the ceiling are restricted.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setSiloedBorrowing enables or disables siloed borrowing for an asset. Existing positions continue; only the rule for new borrows changes.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setBorrowCap sets the maximum total borrow amount for a reserve. Existing borrows remain valid; only new borrows that would exceed the new cap are blocked.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setSupplyCap sets the maximum total supply for a reserve. Existing deposits are unaffected; only new supplies that would exceed the new cap are blocked.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setLiquidationProtocolFee changes the share of liquidation bonus that accrues to the protocol. Future liquidations pay the new fee; outstanding collateral positions and debt are unaffected.

mitigations.label: governance-delayed
mitigations.description: setEModeCategory on the PoolConfigurator sets eMode category parameters (LTV, LT, liquidation bonus, label, oracle). Lowering the liquidation threshold for an active eMode category could make existing positions liquidatable. Requires POOL_ADMIN_ROLE which on the main market requires full governance + 86400s delay.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setAssetCollateralInEMode changes whether an asset is recognized as eMode collateral. Existing positions keep their collateral status; the rule applies only to new eMode configurations.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setAssetBorrowableInEMode changes whether an asset can be borrowed within a given eMode category. Existing eMode positions are unaffected; only future eMode borrow initiations are constrained.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setReserveInterestRateData updates the interest rate strategy parameters (slopes, optimal usage ratio) for a reserve. The new curve governs future interest accrual; it does not restate past accruals or alter outstanding principal.

mitigations.label: emergency-pause

mitigations.label: future-only

mitigations.label: future-only

mitigations.label: oracle-replacement
mitigations.description: setAssetSources replaces the price oracle for one or more assets. A malicious oracle could mass-liquidate borrowers of the affected asset by quoting a price near zero. Bounded by debt at risk on the affected asset; we use the largest current Horizon reserve (aHorRwaRLUSD totalSupply) as a worst-case proxy.

mitigations.label: oracle-replacement
mitigations.description: setFallbackOracle replaces the fallback oracle, used when the primary asset oracle is unavailable. Same liquidation-pressure shape as setAssetSources, bounded by the same Horizon reserve TVS proxy.

mitigations.label: timelock-delayed
mitigations.description: The proxy admin is controlled by the Aave governance Executor (0x5300A1a15135EA4dc7aD5a167152C01EFc9b192A), which requires a full governance proposal vote plus minimum 86400s PayloadsController execution delay before any upgrade can execute. There is no multisig shortcut for these contracts.

mitigations.label: timelock-delayed

There is no multisig shortcut for these contracts.

mitigations.label: rewards config
mitigations.description: configureAssets sets emission parameters (rate, distribution end, transfer strategy, oracle) for reward distributions. A malicious transfer strategy could redirect future reward claims. The impact is limited to rewards not yet claimed by users at the time of the attack.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setDistributionEnd sets the timestamp when reward emissions stop for an asset. Rewards already accrued up to that point are claimable; the change is future-only.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setEmissionPerSecond changes the reward emission rate for an asset going forward. Past accruals are already checkpointed and not retroactively altered.

mitigations.label: rewards redirect
mitigations.description: setTransferStrategy changes the contract responsible for transferring reward tokens to claimants. A malicious transfer strategy could intercept all future reward claims and redirect tokens to an attacker. Impact is bounded by unclaimed accrued rewards at the time of replacement.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setRewardOracle updates the USD price oracle used for reward valuation display purposes. It does not affect the amount of reward tokens distributed or claimable.

mitigations.label: rewards claimer
mitigations.description: setClaimer sets an address that can claim rewards on behalf of another user (claimRewardsOnBehalf). A malicious claimer could claim accrued rewards and redirect them to an arbitrary address. The bound is the total unclaimed rewards accumulated by affected users at the time of call.

mitigations.label: role-expansion
mitigations.description: grantRole adds a new member to an access control role. Granting a sensitive role (e.g., POOL_ADMIN_ROLE, DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE) to a malicious address gives that address all permissions that role entails. On Aave-managed contracts this requires either governance + timelock or multisig approval depending on the specific ACL admin structure.

mitigations.label: defensive
mitigations.description: revokeRole removes an existing role assignment. It cannot grant new capabilities or move funds; it only reduces access. Even in the worst case, revoking a critical operational role disrupts protocol configuration but does not drain user funds.

mitigations.label: minting-authority
mitigations.description: addFacilitator registers a new GHO facilitator with a specified bucket capacity. The new facilitator can then mint GHO up to that capacity. If a malicious address is added as a facilitator, it can mint GHO freely up to its bucket cap, potentially creating unbacked GHO inflation.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: removeFacilitator removes a GHO facilitator, preventing it from minting new GHO. Existing GHO already minted by that facilitator remains in circulation; the operation only stops future minting.

mitigations.label: minting-cap
mitigations.description: setFacilitatorBucketCapacity sets the maximum GHO that a specific facilitator can mint. Increasing the bucket capacity for an existing facilitator enables it to mint more GHO, potentially inflating supply. The maximum GHO mintable equals the new bucket capacity.

mitigations.label: timelock-delayed
mitigations.description: The proxy admin is controlled by the Aave governance Executor (0x5300A1a15135EA4dc7aD5a167152C01EFc9b192A), which requires a full governance proposal vote plus minimum 86400s PayloadsController execution delay before any upgrade can execute. There is no multisig shortcut for these contracts.

mitigations.label: timelock-delayed
mitigations.description: The proxy admin is controlled by the Aave governance Executor (0x5300A1a15135EA4dc7aD5a167152C01EFc9b192A), which requires a full governance proposal vote plus minimum 86400s PayloadsController execution delay before any upgrade can execute. There is no multisig shortcut for these contracts.

mitigations.label: owner-gated GHO facilitator
mitigations.description: mintAndSupply mints GHO (up to the facilitator bucket capacity) and supplies it to the Pool as liquidity. If called by a malicious owner, it could inflate GHO supply up to the bucket cap and create aGHO positions that could be drained. The bound is the current bucket capacity for this facilitator (LidoGhoDirectMinter). The capacity is set on the GhoToken by FACILITATOR_MANAGER_ROLE (Aave Governance only); the GhoBucketSteward can raise it by at most 2× per call with a 1-day cooldown.

mitigations.label: burn-only
mitigations.description: withdrawAndBurn withdraws GHO liquidity that was previously supplied by the GhoDirectMinter itself, then burns it. The GHO is removed from circulation; no user deposits or external funds are touched.

mitigations.label: guardian-change
mitigations.description: updateGuardian replaces the guardian on this LidoGhoDirectMinter. The worst-case fund impact is bounded by what the new guardian can do — mintAndSupply up to this facilitator's bucket capacity. The owner (Aave Governance) can revoke or replace the guardian back at any time.

mitigations.label: admin-transfer
mitigations.description: transferOwnership replaces the current admin/owner with an arbitrary address. If set to a malicious or uncontrolled address, all owner-gated functions are immediately accessible to that address. On contracts under Aave governance, this change requires the standard proposal vote + 86400s execution delay.

mitigations.label: admin-lockout
mitigations.description: renounceOwnership permanently removes the admin/owner, locking all owner-only functions forever. This is an irreversible action that would render governance upgrades and parameter changes impossible. Most Aave contracts accept this through full governance vote + 86400s PayloadsController delay, giving the community time to detect and react.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: updateFee changes the GHO flash-loan fee rate. The new rate applies to future flash loans only; existing positions and balances are unaffected.

mitigations.label: fee-redirect
mitigations.description: updateGhoTreasury changes the destination address for GHO flash loan fees. A malicious treasury address would receive all future fee distributions from the GhoFlashMinter. The immediate impact is bounded by accrued fees; ongoing impact diverts future revenue.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: distributeFeesToTreasury sends accrued flash-loan fees to the configured _ghoTreasury address. It executes a pre-authorized transfer; the destination is set by updateGhoTreasury (a separate function). Called by anyone, it only moves already-accrued fee balances.

mitigations.label: defensive
mitigations.description: revokeRole removes an existing role assignment. It cannot grant new capabilities or move funds; it only reduces access. Even in the worst case, revoking a critical operational role disrupts protocol configuration but does not drain user funds.

mitigations.label: bucket-cap
mitigations.description: Mints new GHO via the facilitator's bucket on the GhoToken (this facilitator labeled `GhoDirectFacilitator GSMs Mainnet`). Each call is bounded by `bucketCapacity - bucketLevel` for this facilitator. The capacity itself is set by FACILITATOR_MANAGER_ROLE (Aave Governance only), not by MINTER_ROLE — so a malicious MINTER_ROLE holder cannot raise their own ceiling.

mitigations.label: rewards config
mitigations.description: configureAssets sets emission parameters (rate, distribution end, transfer strategy, oracle) for reward distributions. A malicious transfer strategy could redirect future reward claims. The impact is limited to rewards not yet claimed by users at the time of the attack.

mitigations.label: slashing risk
mitigations.description: slash transfers a specified amount of staked tokens (stkAAVE or Umbrella stake tokens) from the staking contract to an arbitrary target address, up to the maxSlashablePercentage. The maximum loss equals maxSlashablePercentage × total staked supply. On StakedAaveV3, the slash authority is the Executor (governance). On UmbrellaStakeTokens, the Umbrella controller governs slashing.

mitigations.label: slash-completion
mitigations.description: settleSlashing completes a slashing event by restoring normal staking operations after the slash is finalized. It does not itself transfer funds but must be called after slash to restore staking functionality. Without settleSlashing, the staking contract remains in a post-slash state.

mitigations.label: slash-cap
mitigations.description: setMaxSlashablePercentage sets the maximum fraction of staked tokens that can be slashed in a single event. Increasing this to 100% enables a full stake drain on the next slash call. The actual drain requires a subsequent slash call from the same admin. On StakedAaveV3, governed by the Executor (governance + 86400s delay).

mitigations.label: future-only

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: cooldownOnBehalfOf initiates the withdrawal cooldown timer for a user. It only starts the clock; no funds move, and the user can still cancel or wait for the window. The caller cannot direct where withdrawn funds go.

mitigations.label: redirectable-unstake
mitigations.description: redeemOnBehalf (callable by CLAIM_HELPER_ROLE) redeems staked tokens on behalf of a user and sends them to a specified  address. mitigations.label: reward-redirect

mitigations.label: combined-redirect
mitigations.description: claimRewardsAndRedeemOnBehalf combines reward claiming and stake redemption in one call, both subject to a CLAIM_HELPER-specified destination. A malicious CLAIM_HELPER could redirect both unclaimed rewards and unstaked principal to an arbitrary address.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: claimRewardsAndStakeOnBehalf claims accrued rewards and re-stakes them on the user's behalf. This is a compounding operation that increases the user's stake; no value leaves the staking system or is redirected.

mitigations.label: admin-change

mitigations.label: slashing risk

The maximum loss equals maxSlashablePercentage × total staked supply. On StakedAaveV3, the slash authority is the Executor (governance). On UmbrellaStakeTokens, the Umbrella controller governs slashing.

mitigations.label: slash-completion
mitigations.description: settleSlashing completes a slashing event by restoring normal staking operations after the slash is finalized. It does not itself transfer funds but must be called after slash to restore staking functionality. Without settleSlashing, the staking contract remains in a post-slash state.

mitigations.label: slash-cap

Increasing this to 100% enables a full stake drain on the next slash call. The actual drain requires a subsequent slash call from the same admin. mitigations.label: timelock-delayed

There is no multisig shortcut for these contracts.

mitigations.label: owner-gated GHO facilitator
mitigations.description: mintAndSupply mints GHO (up to the facilitator bucket capacity) and supplies it to the Pool as liquidity. If called by a malicious owner, it could inflate GHO supply up to the bucket cap and create aGHO positions that could be drained. The bound is the current bucket capacity for this facilitator (CoreGhoDirectMinter). The capacity is set on the GhoToken by FACILITATOR_MANAGER_ROLE (Aave Governance only); the GhoBucketSteward can raise it by at most 2× per call with a 1-day cooldown.

mitigations.label: burn-only
mitigations.description: withdrawAndBurn withdraws GHO liquidity that was previously supplied by the GhoDirectMinter itself, then burns it. The GHO is removed from circulation; no user deposits or external funds are touched.

mitigations.label: guardian-change
mitigations.description: updateGuardian replaces the guardian on this CoreGhoDirectMinter. The worst-case fund impact is bounded by what the new guardian can do — mintAndSupply up to this facilitator's bucket capacity. The owner (Aave Governance) can revoke or replace the guardian back at any time.

mitigations.label: admin-transfer
mitigations.description: transferOwnership replaces the current admin/owner with an arbitrary address. If set to a malicious or uncontrolled address, all owner-gated functions are immediately accessible to that address. On contracts under Aave governance, this change requires the standard proposal vote + 86400s execution delay.

mitigations.label: admin-lockout
mitigations.description: renounceOwnership permanently removes the admin/owner, locking all owner-only functions forever. This is an irreversible action that would render governance upgrades and parameter changes impossible. Most Aave contracts accept this through full governance vote + 86400s PayloadsController delay, giving the community time to detect and react.

mitigations.label: trusted-sender
mitigations.description: approveSenders adds trusted sender addresses for cross-chain messaging. An approved sender on the source chain can submit messages that the destination chain will process as governance actions. Adding a malicious sender compromises cross-chain governance integrity.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: removeSenders removes a cross-chain sender from the trusted set. This is a defensive revocation; in-flight messages from that sender may fail, but it does not endanger existing on-chain state.

mitigations.label: bridge-config

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: disableBridgeAdapters removes a bridge adapter from the send-side. It reduces the available redundancy for outgoing cross-chain messages but does not affect already-delivered messages or on-chain state.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: updateOptimalBandwidthByChain sets the preferred bridge adapter count for a destination chain. It is a routing preference setting with no direct fund impact.

mitigations.label: bridge-security
mitigations.description: updateConfirmations changes the number of bridge adapter confirmations required to accept a cross-chain message. Setting this to 1 would allow a single compromised bridge adapter to deliver arbitrary governance payloads without requiring consensus from other adapters.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: updateMessagesValidityTimestamp sets the earliest timestamp at which inbound messages are considered valid. Messages older than this threshold are rejected (replay protection). It is a security hygiene parameter.

mitigations.label: bridge-config
mitigations.description: allowReceiverBridgeAdapters adds a trusted bridge adapter for receiving cross-chain messages. mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: disallowReceiverBridgeAdapters prevents a bridge adapter from delivering inbound messages. Existing delivered messages are already processed; only future deliveries via that adapter are blocked.

mitigations.label: message-relay
mitigations.description: forwardMessage sends a cross-chain message through the CrossChainController. An authorized sender could relay arbitrary payloads to destination chains where they would be processed as governance messages, potentially executing unauthorized changes.

mitigations.label: bridge-gated
mitigations.description: receiveCrossChainMessage processes inbound cross-chain governance messages. It is gated by a check that the sender is the trusted CrossChainController. A compromised or spoofed CrossChainController could deliver arbitrary governance payloads, bypassing the intended source-chain governance process.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: retryEnvelope re-attempts delivery of a failed cross-chain message envelope. It only retransmits already-created messages; no new instructions or value transfers are introduced.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: retryTransaction re-attempts execution of a cross-chain transaction that was delivered but failed on execution. It cannot create new transactions or change their parameters.

mitigations.label: trusted-sender
mitigations.description: approveSenders adds trusted sender addresses for cross-chain messaging. An approved sender on the source chain can submit messages that the destination chain will process as governance actions. Adding a malicious sender compromises cross-chain governance integrity.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: removeSenders removes a cross-chain sender from the trusted set. This is a defensive revocation; in-flight messages from that sender may fail, but it does not endanger existing on-chain state.

mitigations.label: bridge-config
mitigations.description: enableBridgeAdapters adds bridge adapters for sending cross-chain messages. A malicious adapter could fail to deliver messages (griefing) or manipulate message content. However, message authenticity is also verified by the destination chain's confirmations threshold.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: disableBridgeAdapters removes a bridge adapter from the send-side. It reduces the available redundancy for outgoing cross-chain messages but does not affect already-delivered messages or on-chain state.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: updateOptimalBandwidthByChain sets the preferred bridge adapter count for a destination chain. It is a routing preference setting with no direct fund impact.

mitigations.label: bridge-security

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: updateMessagesValidityTimestamp sets the earliest timestamp at which inbound messages are considered valid. Messages older than this threshold are rejected (replay protection). It is a security hygiene parameter.

mitigations.label: bridge-config

mitigations.label: future-only

mitigations.label: inert on mainnet
mitigations.description: Forwards into the CrossChainController's solveEmergency selector, which only exists on the L2 receiver-side variant (CrossChainControllerWithEmergencyMode + EmergencyConsumer). Aave's Ethereum-mainnet CrossChainController is the governance forwarder side and does not inherit EmergencyConsumer, so the underlying selector is absent and the call reverts. The 5-of-9 SOLVE_EMERGENCY_ROLE multisig has no fund-impacting path through this function on mainnet. Re-evaluate if the mainnet CrossChainController proxy (0xEd42...b0e1) is ever upgraded to an emergency-mode-enabled implementation.

mitigations.label: inert on mainnet
mitigations.description: Legacy variant of solveEmergency targeting an older receiver-side interface. Same outcome as solveEmergency on Ethereum mainnet: the underlying CrossChainController never implements either selector, so the call reverts. Re-evaluate if the mainnet CrossChainController proxy (0xEd42...b0e1) is ever upgraded to an emergency-mode-enabled implementation.

mitigations.label: future-only

User principal stays in the aToken contract via scaled balances; the controller is only consulted via handleAction(...) on transfer/mint/burn for reward accounting. Worst-case effect from a malicious controller: (a) revert on every transfer/mint/burn → temporary DoS, recoverable by replacing the controller, and (b) divert any actively-streaming reward emissions until rotated. Neither path can extract user principal.

mitigations.label: rewards config
mitigations.description: configureAssets sets emission parameters (rate, distribution end, transfer strategy, oracle) for reward distributions. A malicious transfer strategy could redirect future reward claims. The impact is limited to rewards not yet claimed by users at the time of the attack.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setDistributionEnd sets the timestamp when reward emissions stop for an asset. Rewards already accrued up to that point are claimable; the change is future-only.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setEmissionPerSecond changes the reward emission rate for an asset going forward. Past accruals are already checkpointed and not retroactively altered.

mitigations.label: rewards redirect
mitigations.description: setTransferStrategy changes the contract responsible for transferring reward tokens to claimants. A malicious transfer strategy could intercept all future reward claims and redirect tokens to an attacker. Impact is bounded by unclaimed accrued rewards at the time of replacement.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setRewardOracle updates the USD price oracle used for reward valuation display purposes. It does not affect the amount of reward tokens distributed or claimable.

mitigations.label: rewards claimer
mitigations.description: setClaimer sets an address that can claim rewards on behalf of another user (claimRewardsOnBehalf). A malicious claimer could claim accrued rewards and redirect them to an arbitrary address. The bound is the total unclaimed rewards accumulated by affected users at the time of call.

mitigations.label: agent-replacement

mitigations.label: future-only

mitigations.label: future-only

mitigations.label: oracle-publisher
mitigations.description: addAuthorizedSender grants an address the right to publish risk parameter updates to the RiskOracle. A malicious authorized sender could publish extreme parameter updates (e.g., risk slopes, caps) that downstream agents would then inject into the protocol.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: removeAuthorizedSender removes a publisher from the RiskOracle whitelist. Future updates from that address are rejected; already-recorded updates in the oracle are not deleted.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: addUpdateType registers a new risk parameter update type identifier in the RiskOracle. It is a data-classification operation with no direct effect on protocol parameters or balances.

mitigations.label: parameter-injection
mitigations.description: publishRiskParameterUpdate records a new risk parameter value in the RiskOracle, which agents then inject into the protocol. An authorized sender publishing extreme values (e.g., very high borrow rates or zero caps) would affect the protocol on the next agent execution.

mitigations.label: parameter-injection
mitigations.description: publishBulkRiskParameterUpdates records multiple risk parameters simultaneously. Same risk as publishRiskParameterUpdate — an authorized sender could inject a coordinated set of extreme parameter values.

mitigations.label: oracle-validated future-only

AgentHub reads parameter updates exclusively from the RiskOracle (which records authorized publisher updates) and applies the governance-configured agentContext stored in AgentHub storage. The actual parameter change (e.g., interest rate update, cap change, eMode update) is future-only and bounded by the agent's validation logic. The agentContext target address is set by governance and cannot be changed by keepers.

Changes affect only the ceiling for future new positions.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: updateRates writes the per-asset interest-rate parameters consumed by Aave V3's variable-rate strategy. The new rate applies to *future* interest accrual only — existing principal and previously-accrued interest are not retroactively repriced. No path from this call moves committed deposits or debt. The RiskCouncil's per-call deltas are also bounded (rate ±100 bps, slope2 ±2000 bps, optimalUsage ±300 bps) with a 3-day per-asset cooldown, but the future-only nature is the load-bearing reason there is no fund impact.

Each LTV/LiquidationThreshold/LiquidationBonus/DebtCeiling change is debounced per-asset by a 3-day cooldown via _timelocks; the same parameter on the same asset cannot be re-pushed until 259200s have passed.

mitigations.label: bounded-collateral-step
mitigations.description: Per cooldown LTV, LiquidationThreshold, and LiquidationBonus may each move max 50 bps in absolute terms (0.5pp); DebtCeiling may move max 20% relative. A 0.5pp LiquidationThreshold drop can mass-liquidate positions whose health factor sits in the 1.000-1.005 band, but the bound prevents arbitrary cascade liquidations in a single push. Configurable via setRiskConfig (owner-only = DAO Executor). The cap is a heuristic upper bound (2.5% of Aave Mainnet aUSDC TVS as a proxy for protocol-scale at-risk debt) on the at-risk debt that historically sits in the HF 1.000-1.005 band on Aave V3 markets — it is not on-chain enforced. The DAO Executor can revoke RISK_COUNCIL or tighten setRiskConfig within the 3-day cooldown to react to abuse.
Each LST oracle price-cap update is debounced per-oracle by a 3-day cooldown via _timelocks.

mitigations.label: bounded-price-cap-step
mitigations.description: Per cooldown maxYearlyRatioGrowthPercent may move max 5% relative; snapshotRatio is forced to be <= currentRatio (UpdateNotInRange revert), so the cap can only become MORE conservative, never more permissive. Effect: a tighter cap depresses oracle reads on LST collateral, which can force liquidation of positions sitting near liquidation threshold. No path exists to over-value LSTs. Configurable via setRiskConfig (owner-only = DAO Executor). The cap is a heuristic upper bound (2.5% of Aave Mainnet aUSDC TVS as a proxy for protocol-scale at-risk debt) on at-risk LST-collateralised debt that would become liquidatable from a single bounded price-cap tightening — it is not on-chain enforced.
Each stable oracle price-cap update is debounced per-oracle by a 3-day cooldown via _timelocks.

mitigations.label: bounded-price-cap-step
mitigations.description: Per cooldown stable priceCap may move max 50 bps relative. Effect: same liquidation-pressure shape as the LST cap when set tighter than market price. Configurable via setRiskConfig (owner-only = DAO Executor). The cap is a heuristic upper bound (2.5% of Aave Mainnet aUSDC TVS as a proxy for protocol-scale at-risk debt) on at-risk stablecoin-collateralised debt that would become liquidatable from a single bounded price-cap tightening — it is not on-chain enforced.

mitigations.label: defensive
mitigations.description: pause stops new stake/unstake operations for the staking contract. Existing staked balances are not moved, slashed, or locked permanently; users can resume operations after unpause. This is a standard emergency safety mechanism.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: unpause re-enables stake/unstake operations. It only restores functionality that was previously suspended; no funds move and no permissions are elevated.

mitigations.label: slashing risk
mitigations.description: slash transfers a specified amount of staked tokens (stkAAVE or Umbrella stake tokens) from the staking contract to an arbitrary target address, up to the maxSlashablePercentage. The maximum loss equals maxSlashablePercentage × total staked supply. On StakedAaveV3, the slash authority is the Executor (governance). On UmbrellaStakeTokens, the Umbrella controller governs slashing.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setUnstakeWindow changes the post-cooldown withdrawal window. Active cooldowns complete normally; only future cooldowns use the new window.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setCooldown changes the cooldown period for the UmbrellaStakeToken. Existing in-progress cooldown timers are not changed; only newly initiated cooldowns use the updated period.

mitigations.label: admin-lockout
mitigations.description: renounceOwnership permanently removes the admin/owner, locking all owner-only functions forever. This is an irreversible action that would render governance upgrades and parameter changes impossible. Most Aave contracts accept this through full governance vote + 86400s PayloadsController delay, giving the community time to detect and react.

mitigations.label: admin-transfer
mitigations.description: transferOwnership replaces the current admin/owner with an arbitrary address. If set to a malicious or uncontrolled address, all owner-gated functions are immediately accessible to that address. On contracts under Aave governance, this change requires the standard proposal vote + 86400s execution delay.

mitigations.label: pause-bound
mitigations.description: setPaused on a StataTokenV2 wrapper blocks Stata-level deposits/withdrawals; the underlying aToken position remains untouched and aToken holders are unaffected. No fund extraction or devaluation. Worst-case impact is bounded by the wrapper's totalAssets (a strict subset of the main Aave V3 Ethereum market TVS); we cap conservatively at the whole-market TVS as the tightest fieldRef-able upper bound.

mitigations.label: rescue function
mitigations.description: emergencyTokenTransfer sends any ERC-20 token held by the contract to an arbitrary address. It is intended as a recovery function for tokens accidentally sent to the contract. The maximum loss is bounded by the contract's own token balances at the time of call.

mitigations.label: rescue function
mitigations.description: emergencyEtherTransfer sends native ETH held by the contract to an arbitrary address. The maximum loss is bounded by the contract's ETH balance at the time of call.

mitigations.label: deficit required
mitigations.description: Cannot drain on demand. slash() requires the Aave Pool to already hold an actual reserve deficit (poolDeficit > deficitOffset + pendingDeficit). Without real bad debt the configs change nothing and no funds move.

mitigations.label: fee ≤ 100%
mitigations.description: Per-config liquidationFee is capped at ONE_HUNDRED_PERCENT in the validator, so any single slash can extract at most 2× the actual deficit (deficit + 100% fee).

mitigations.label: fixed recipient
mitigations.description: SLASHED_FUNDS_RECIPIENT is set once in the initializer (currently the Aave Collector) and has no setter. The admin cannot redirect slashed funds to itself — worst case is forced loss to a stake token's depositors with proceeds flowing to the Aave DAO treasury.

mitigations.label: deficit required
mitigations.description: Lowering the offset only exposes pre-existing pool deficit to slashing; it cannot create deficit. Bounded by the actual on-chain Pool deficit at slash time.

mitigations.label: fixed recipient
mitigations.description: Slashed funds always flow to the immutable SLASHED_FUNDS_RECIPIENT (Aave Collector). Admin cannot redirect proceeds.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: removeSlashingConfigs removes a slashing configuration from the Umbrella system. The corresponding staking token no longer participates in deficit coverage; existing user staked positions are not immediately affected.

mitigations.label: empty on create
mitigations.description: Newly deployed stake tokens hold no deposits. Drain only materializes via a separate updateSlashingConfigs call, which has its own bounds.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setCooldownStk changes the cooldown duration for a specific staking token in the Umbrella system. Existing in-progress cooldowns are not altered.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: setUnstakeWindowStk changes the unstake window for a specific staking token. Future cooldowns use the new window.

mitigations.label: principal protected
mitigations.description: maxRescue caps the staked asset at balanceOf - totalAssets (dust only); principal cannot leave the contract.

mitigations.label: no ETH held
mitigations.description: Stake tokens are ERC4626 vaults that don't intentionally hold ETH; drain target is essentially zero.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: pauseStk pauses new stake/unstake operations for a specific staking token. Existing staked balances are not moved or slashed; only new operations are blocked.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: unpauseStk re-enables stake/unstake operations. It restores future functionality.

mitigations.label: rewards config
mitigations.description: configureAssetWithRewards sets up reward distributions for a specific Umbrella staking asset. Misconfiguration could redirect future reward emissions to attacker-controlled transfer strategies.

mitigations.label: rewards claimer
mitigations.description: setClaimer sets an address that can claim rewards on behalf of another user (claimRewardsOnBehalf). A malicious claimer could claim accrued rewards and redirect them to an arbitrary address. The bound is the total unclaimed rewards accumulated by affected users at the time of call.

mitigations.label: rescue function
mitigations.description: emergencyTokenTransfer sends any ERC-20 token held by the contract to an arbitrary address. It is intended as a recovery function for tokens accidentally sent to the contract. The maximum loss is bounded by the contract's own token balances at the time of call.

mitigations.label: rescue function
mitigations.description: emergencyEtherTransfer sends native ETH held by the contract to an arbitrary address. The maximum loss is bounded by the contract's ETH balance at the time of call.

mitigations.label: rewards config
mitigations.description: configureRewards sets up reward distribution configurations for the Umbrella rewards controller. A malicious configuration could redirect future reward emissions to attacker-controlled addresses or block legitimate reward distributions.

mitigations.label: collector-bound
mitigations.description: swap routes Collector tokens through Milkman with the receiver hardcoded to the Collector. The pair must be in swapApprovedToken (owner-set), per-call amount is capped by tokenBudget[fromToken] (owner-set), and slippage is hard-capped at MAX_SLIPPAGE = 10% (immutable). Worst-case loss per swap is the swapped amount × 10% to MEV/bad fill; bounded across all tokens by 10% of the live Aave V3 Collector treasury size.

mitigations.label: collector-bound
mitigations.description: limitSwap submits a Cowswap limit order with the receiver hardcoded to the Collector. The pair must be swappablePair-approved (owner-set) and the per-call amount is capped by tokenBudget[fromToken]. The fixed amountOut is the worst-case execution price; the structural slippage bound is the same 10% MAX_SLIPPAGE ceiling. Bounded across all tokens by 10% of the live Aave V3 Collector treasury size.

mitigations.label: collector-bound
mitigations.description: twapSwap submits a TWAP order over numParts × partSellAmount with the receiver hardcoded to the Collector. Pair must be swappablePair-approved and total amount is capped by tokenBudget[fromToken] when called by the guardian. Per-part minPartLimit gives a slippage floor; aggregate worst-case loss is bounded by 10% of the live Aave V3 Collector treasury size.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: cancelSwap cancels a pending Milkman swap order. It unwinds an outstanding swap intent without moving funds; the swap simply does not execute.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: cancelLimitSwap cancels a pending limit order. Same as cancelSwap — no funds move, the order is voided.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: cancelTwapSwap cancels a pending TWAP swap. Same pattern — the order is voided, no funds move.

mitigations.label: collector-bound
mitigations.description: rescueToken sweeps any ERC-20 stuck on the steward back to the Collector. The receiver is hardcoded to the Collector, so the action is restorative — it cannot move funds to a caller-chosen destination. The steward only holds tokens transiently between swap submission and settlement, so the practical balance swept is small.

mitigations.label: budget-expansion
mitigations.description: increaseTokenBudget raises the maximum amount of a token that the SwapSteward is authorized to swap. A larger budget increases the maximum possible loss from a swap attack. Controlled by the guardian or owner.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: decreaseTokenBudget reduces the maximum token budget for a swappable pair. It is a defensive restriction that limits future swap exposure.

mitigations.label: swap-config
mitigations.description: setSwappablePair configures a new token pair as eligible for swapping. Adding a pair with a malicious oracle or price checker could enable value extraction during swaps.

mitigations.label: price-manipulation
mitigations.description: setTokenOracle changes the price oracle used to validate swap fairness. A malicious oracle could report prices that allow swaps at unfavorable rates, enabling value extraction from the Collector.

mitigations.label: slippage-bypass
mitigations.description: setPriceChecker changes the contract that validates swap prices. A malicious price checker could approve swaps at any exchange rate, enabling full extraction of swapped token amounts from the Collector.

mitigations.label: slippage-bypass
mitigations.description: setLimitOrderPriceChecker changes the price validation contract for limit orders. A malicious checker could bypass slippage protection.

mitigations.label: swap-executor
mitigations.description: setMilkman changes the swap execution contract. A malicious Milkman could execute swaps to attacker-controlled addresses or at unfavorable rates.

mitigations.label: swap-executor
mitigations.description: setRelayer changes the order relayer that broadcasts swap intents. A malicious relayer could refuse to execute swaps (griefing) but cannot steal funds directly.

mitigations.label: admin-lockout
mitigations.description: renounceOwnership permanently removes the admin/owner, locking all owner-only functions forever. This is an irreversible action that would render governance upgrades and parameter changes impossible. Most Aave contracts accept this through full governance vote + 86400s PayloadsController delay, giving the community time to detect and react.

mitigations.label: admin-transfer
mitigations.description: transferOwnership replaces the current admin/owner with an arbitrary address. If set to a malicious or uncontrolled address, all owner-gated functions are immediately accessible to that address. On contracts under Aave governance, this change requires the standard proposal vote + 86400s execution delay.

mitigations.label: collector-bound
mitigations.description: updateGuardian replaces the guardian address. The new guardian inherits guardian-only permissions on this steward: swap, limitSwap, twapSwap, cancel*, and rescueToken — all of which route funds to the Collector with hardcoded receiver and 10% MAX_SLIPPAGE cap. Bounded by the swap-loss ceiling (10% of the live Aave V3 Collector treasury size).

mitigations.label: collector-bound
mitigations.description: depositV3 supplies Collector tokens to a governance-approved Aave V3 pool. The aToken receipt is minted to the Collector and the target pool must be in approvedV3Pools (set only by governance via approvePool), so funds cannot leave the Aave Collector. Worst-case loss is concentration risk if a chosen approved pool is later compromised; bounded by the live Aave V3 Collector treasury size.

mitigations.label: collector-bound
mitigations.description: withdrawV3 redeems an aToken position held by the Collector back to the underlying token, with the receiver hardcoded to the Collector. Pool must be in approvedV3Pools (governance-set). Funds round-trip to and from the Collector; no caller-controlled destination. Bounded by the live Aave V3 Collector treasury size.

mitigations.label: collector-bound
mitigations.description: withdrawV2 unwinds a legacy V2 aToken position; the underlying is returned to the Collector. Pool must be in approvedV2Pools (governance-set) and the receiver is hardcoded to the Collector. Bounded by the Collector's V2 holdings (subset of live treasury); no caller-controlled destination.

mitigations.label: collector-bound
mitigations.description: migrateV2toV3 unwinds a V2 position and supplies the proceeds to a V3 pool, with the new aToken minted to the Collector. Both pools must be governance-approved and funds never leave the Collector. Worst-case is concentration into one approved pool that is later compromised; bounded by the live Aave V3 Collector treasury size.

mitigations.label: collector-bound
mitigations.description: migrateBetweenV3 redeems one V3 aToken and supplies the proceeds to another V3 pool, with the new aToken minted to the Collector. Both pools must be in approvedV3Pools (governance-set). Funds never leave the Collector; worst-case is concentration into one approved pool that is later compromised, bounded by the live Aave V3 Collector treasury size.

mitigations.label: collector-bound
mitigations.description: updateGuardian replaces the guardian address. The new guardian inherits the steward's guardian-only permissions (depositV3, withdrawV3, withdrawV2, migrateV2toV3, migrateBetweenV3) but those functions all route funds through approvedV3Pools/approvedV2Pools (governance-set) with the receiver hardcoded to the Collector — funds cannot leave the Aave Collector. Bounded by the live Aave V3 Collector treasury size.

mitigations.label: pool-approval
mitigations.description: approvePool adds a pool to the list of pools the PoolExposureSteward is authorized to interact with. A malicious pool could steal funds when the steward deposits.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: revokePool removes a pool from the set of pools the PoolExposureSteward is allowed to interact with. It is a defensive restriction that reduces the steward's operational scope.

mitigations.label: admin-lockout
mitigations.description: renounceOwnership permanently removes the admin/owner, locking all owner-only functions forever. This is an irreversible action that would render governance upgrades and parameter changes impossible. Most Aave contracts accept this through full governance vote + 86400s PayloadsController delay, giving the community time to detect and react.

mitigations.label: admin-transfer
mitigations.description: transferOwnership replaces the current admin/owner with an arbitrary address. If set to a malicious or uncontrolled address, all owner-gated functions are immediately accessible to that address. On contracts under Aave governance, this change requires the standard proposal vote + 86400s execution delay.

mitigations.label: operational
mitigations.description: renewAllowance sets a token approval from the ClinicSteward to the Pool (type(uint256).max). It is a prerequisite for repayment operations that benefit the protocol; the approval itself does not transfer any tokens.

mitigations.label: budget-bounded
mitigations.description: ClinicSteward A.batchRepayBadDebt pulls funds from the AaveCollector treasury and pays them to the Aave Pool to either repay bad debt on insolvent positions (batchRepayBadDebt) or trigger liquidations of insolvent positions (batchLiquidate). Both paths route excess back to the Collector. The total Collector outflow per call is debited from `availableBudget` (USD, 8-decimal Aave oracle units) via `_decreaseAvailableBudget`; once the budget is exhausted the function reverts with AvailableBudgetExceeded. The budget can only be replenished by setAvailableBudget which is DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE-gated (DAO Executor) - the CLEANUP_ROLE bot/Safe cannot raise its own ceiling.

mitigations.label: budget-bounded
mitigations.description: ClinicSteward A.batchLiquidate pulls funds from the AaveCollector treasury and pays them to the Aave Pool to either repay bad debt on insolvent positions (batchRepayBadDebt) or trigger liquidations of insolvent positions (batchLiquidate). Both paths route excess back to the Collector. The total Collector outflow per call is debited from `availableBudget` (USD, 8-decimal Aave oracle units) via `_decreaseAvailableBudget`; once the budget is exhausted the function reverts with AvailableBudgetExceeded. The budget can only be replenished by setAvailableBudget which is DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE-gated (DAO Executor) - the CLEANUP_ROLE bot/Safe cannot raise its own ceiling.

mitigations.label: budget-cap only
mitigations.description: setAvailableBudget sets a USD-denominated ceiling on how much the ClinicSteward can spend on bad-debt repayment and liquidation operations. It does not itself move any funds. Actual transfers only occur when the CLEANUP_ROLE calls batchRepayBadDebt or batchLiquidate, which are bounded by actual on-chain bad debt and liquidatable positions.

mitigations.label: transfer-authority
mitigations.description: grantAuthorizedTransferRole grants an address the ability to call transferRwaAToken. A malicious authorized address could then transfer RWA aTokens from any holder to any destination.

mitigations.label: rwa-transfer-grant
mitigations.description: grantAuthorizedTransferRole grants AUTHORIZED_TRANSFER_ROLE for a single RWA aToken. The role's holder can call transferRwaAToken to move that aToken's balance from any holder to any destination. Per-call impact is bounded by the affected RWA aToken's totalSupply; we use the largest current Horizon RWA aToken (aHorRwaUSCC totalSupply) as a worst-case proxy.

mitigations.label: future-only
mitigations.description: revokeAuthorizedTransferRole removes an address's permission to call transferRwaAToken. Existing aToken balances are unaffected; the revoked address can no longer initiate future transfers.

mitigations.label: authorized-transfer
mitigations.description: transferRwaAToken transfers RWA aTokens from one address to another. Only callable by authorized addresses (grantAuthorizedTransferRole). A malicious authorized address could transfer tokens from any holder without consent.

mitigations.label: rwa-direct-transfer
mitigations.description: transferRwaAToken (onlyRole AUTHORIZED_TRANSFER_ROLE per aToken) moves a single RWA aToken's balance between any two addresses. Per-call impact is bounded by that aToken's totalSupply; we use the largest current Horizon RWA aToken (aHorRwaUSCC totalSupply) as a worst-case proxy.
